key_insight: SciPhyRL turns portfolio optimization into a physics-informed, data-driven control problem by solving a pathwise Hamilton–Jacobi equation directly from historical trajectories.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a practical offline RL recipe for institutional trading that explicitly accounts for transaction costs, turnover, and horizon-dependent execution effects.
method: Formulate portfolio choice in continuous time with an extended state that includes cumulative costs.
Project the HJB equation onto observed paths and solve it with a PINN in one offline pass.
Recast control as a discrete target holding and apply a quadratic price-impact cost model.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on a 14-asset ETF universe using an engineered oracle signal, where the learned Gibbs policy delivers strong out-of-sample Sharpe improvements over static and myopic baselines.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the engineered signal and on how well the quadratic impact model matches real market microstructure.
paper_title: SciPhy Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimization
